description: AWS Deployment — Pulumi infrastructure, API Gateway authorizer, production readiness

tasks.title: Pulumi AWS Infrastructure Stacks
tasks.prompt:
Implement the Pulumi infrastructure (M0.2) as specified in SNAPP-TRD.md WU-0.2.

Create src/Snapp.Infrastructure/Pulumi/ with:

1. Program.cs — Pulumi entry point
2. AwsStack.cs — all AWS resources:
   - 6 DynamoDB tables (snapp-users, snapp-networks, snapp-content, snapp-intel, snapp-tx, snapp-notif) with all GSIs, PAY_PER_REQUEST, Streams, TTL
   - S3 buckets (snapp-media with CORS, snapp-client for Blazor static hosting)
   - CloudFront distribution → snapp-client bucket, /api/* → API Gateway
   - API Gateway (HTTP API) with routes per service, Lambda integrations
   - KMS symmetric key for PII encryption (key policy: Lambda roles only)
   - SES domain verification + configuration set
   - IAM roles (one per Lambda, least-privilege: each scoped to its table(s) + KMS/SES as needed)
   - CloudWatch log groups (30-day retention) + alarms (error rate >5%, p99 >3s)

3. Pulumi config: domain, environment (dev/staging/prod), sesFromAddress

4. Lambda deployment: each service published as Native AOT zip, deployed via Pulumi. Use the existing Dockerfiles as reference for build steps.

Test: `pulumi preview` succeeds. Document the deployment steps in CONTRIBUTING.md.

Note: do NOT deploy to a real AWS account. Just verify the Pulumi code compiles and preview works.
tasks.done_when: Pulumi stack compiles. pulumi preview succeeds. All resources defined. Deployment documented.

tasks.title: API Gateway Authorizer Lambda
tasks.prompt:
Implement the API Gateway Authorizer (M1.4) as specified in SNAPP-TRD.md WU-1.4.

Create Snapp.Service.Authorizer:

1. Lambda function (raw handler, not Minimal API):
   - Input: APIGatewayHttpApiV2ProxyRequest
   - Extract Authorization: Bearer {token}
   - Validate JWT: RS256 signature (public key from Secrets Manager, cached in static field), expiry, issuer, audience
   - Return Allow/Deny IAM policy with context: { userId, emailHash }
   - Cache authorizer result for min(token remaining TTL, 300s)

2. Exclude from authorization (configured in API Gateway, not in this Lambda):
   - POST /api/auth/magic-link, /api/auth/validate, /api/auth/refresh

3. Unit tests: valid JWT → Allow, expired → Deny, wrong signature → Deny, missing header → Deny

This Lambda is prod-only — it replaces Kong's JWT plugin in the AWS environment.
tasks.done_when: Authorizer Lambda compiles. Unit tests pass for all JWT validation scenarios.

tasks.title: Production readiness checklist and CI/CD for AWS
tasks.prompt:
Prepare production deployment infrastructure.

1. Update .github/workflows/deploy.yml:
   - Trigger: manual dispatch or merge to main with [deploy] tag
   - Steps: build all services with Native AOT, run tests, pulumi up to staging, run E2E against staging, manual approval gate, pulumi up to prod

2. Create scripts/deploy-aws.sh:
   - Build all Lambda zips (dotnet publish with Native AOT)
   - Run pulumi up with selected stack (staging/prod)
   - Invalidate CloudFront cache
   - Verify deployment (health checks on all API endpoints)

3. Create docs/PRODUCTION-READINESS.md checklist:
   - [ ] All integration tests pass locally
   - [ ] Pulumi preview clean
   - [ ] KMS key created
   - [ ] SES domain verified
   - [ ] SSL certificate in ACM
   - [ ] DNS configured
   - [ ] Environment variables set in Pulumi config
   - [ ] Secrets in Secrets Manager (JWT signing key)
   - [ ] CloudWatch alarms configured
   - [ ] First deployment to staging verified

4. Update CONTRIBUTING.md with AWS deployment section.
tasks.done_when: Deploy workflow, deploy script, and production readiness checklist all created. Documented.

tasks.title: Final integration validation
tasks.prompt:
Final validation. Run exactly these steps:

1. `dotnet build snapp.sln` — record pass/fail
2. `dotnet test snapp.sln --verbosity quiet` — record per-project pass/fail counts
3. `docker compose -f src/Snapp.Infrastructure/Docker/docker-compose.yml ps` — record container status
4. Write .claude/reports/final-validation.md with:
   - Build result
   - Test table (project, passed, failed, total) + grand total
   - Docker container table (name, status)
   - Feature inventory: list each sprint (S1-S16) with one-line description of what it delivered
   - Any failing tests: list test name + error
   - Verdict: PASS if build clean + all tests pass + all containers running
5. `git add -A && git commit -m '[S16-004] Final validation'`

Do NOT re-run tests, investigate failures, fix code, or do manual walkthroughs. Build, test, report, commit.
